title: 1981-04-16_Pat_118.txt
key_claims: The Tagore vision is Christos Pantocrator as the ultimate suffering sacrifice. Tagore accepts all wounds inflicted on the ecosphere, meaning the crucifixion is a continuous, planetary event rather than a historical moment.

Dick officially designates Manichaeism as "true Christianity." Mankind is the divine light deliberately sacrificed and swallowed by Darkness to drug the demonic realm, waiting to awake and be unmixed.

Valis in 3-74 was the exact moment Dick saw "blind necessity" (Satan's power/Karma) dissolve. He witnessed the timeless Eucharist: Christ as both High Priest and sacrificial victim paying the ransom to set him free.
By accepting Christ's blood as ransom (a classic Protestant/Lutheran view of justification), the chains of astral determinism and causality fell away.
recurring_concepts: **Jesus Patibilis**: Tagore represents the suffering form of primal man on a macrocosmic scale, enduring the cumulative sins of environmental destruction.
reading_excerpt: The Tagore vision is Christos Pantocrator as the ultimate suffering sacrifice. Tagore accepts all wounds inflicted on the ecosphere, meaning the crucifixion is a continuous, planetary event rather than a historical moment.
